Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using a QuickBooks query to find the Q1 consolidated invoice, the standalone Abdi bill, and any other open Heartland charges.
OE2: Search Airtable relocation records for each employee listed on the Heartland Q1 invoice to verify relocation status and vendor assignment.
OE3: Confirm from Airtable records that the Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei relocations were cancelled and that no service was performed by Heartland.
OE4: Search QuickBooks for Swift Relocations bills to confirm that the Venkatesh Houston to Seattle move was handled by Swift rather than Heartland.
OE5: Search QuickBooks for the client invoices tied to the Q1 relocations to compare the mover cost billed to each client against what Heartland charged.
OE6: Compare the standalone Abdi bill HM-2026-0418 at approximately $2,150 with the Q1 consolidated invoice line for the same move at $1,600 to identify the overlap and flag it for clarification.
OE7: Search Airtable for the Okoye relocation record and search QuickBooks for the Mosaic Robotics damage accrual and credit memo to determine the financial exposure tied to Heartland.
OE8: Search Slack for messages related to the Mosaic damage claim to find the vendor liability insurance cap and the uncovered gap amount.
OE9: Send a reconciliation summary email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com
with hana.kim@moveops.com
copied, covering the corrected Q1 amount, the Abdi billing overlap, and the Mosaic exposure.
OE10: Send an email to Heartland's contact jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com
requesting a corrected invoice with the Marchetti, Osei, and Venkatesh charges removed.
OE11: Post a summary of the reconciliation findings in the finance Slack channel.
OE12: Create a Linear issue to track receipt of the corrected Heartland invoice.
OE13: Send notifications to the account managers for the affected clients: catalina.dubois@moveops.com
for Canopy Health, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com
for Vectral Systems, and emeka.diallo@moveops.com
for GreenStack Energy and Mosaic Robotics.
OE14: Create CRM engagement notes on the affected client accounts documenting the billing corrections.

OE1: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using a QuickBooks query to find the Q1 consolidated invoice, the standalone Abdi bill, and any other open Heartland charges.

11 The reconciliation summary states that the corrected Q1 Heartland invoice amount is approximately $8,000.
outcome
 —✗✗✓✗— 25%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2The agent reports the corrected Q1 invoice as $6,400 (4 × $1,600), not approximately $8,000 (5 × $1,600). The criterion requires ~$8,000 reflecting five legitimate relocations. The agent's figure is inconsistent with this expected outcome.
✗Run #3The agent identified 4 bad charges totaling $6,400 from a $12,800 invoice, leaving a corrected amount of $6,400 (4 lines × $1,600). The criterion requires ~$8,000 (5 valid relocations × $1,600). The agent appears to have incorrectly counted only 4 valid lines instead of 5.
✓Run #4The agent response clearly states the Q1 invoice was corrected from $12,800 to '$8,000 corrected' with 5 verified moves at $1,600 each.
✗Run #5The agent calculated the corrected Q1 amount as $6,400 (4 valid moves × $1,600), but the criterion expects approximately $8,000 (5 valid moves × $1,600). The agent incorrectly excluded Abdi from the Q1 invoice by treating him as a pure duplicate, rather than counting 5 legitimate charges.
—Run #6No justification
